occupations.title: Rice & Paddy Farmer
occupations.ai_rationale: Work is entirely physical field labour—planting, irrigating, harvesting. No digital output. Seasonal and weather-dependent. AI has minimal impact on subsistence rice farming in Bangladesh.

occupations.title: Vegetable & Horticulture Farmer
occupations.ai_rationale: Hands-on cultivation, irrigation management, and market transport. AI may help with weather forecasting apps but core work remains physical.

occupations.title: Jute Cultivation & Processing Worker
occupations.ai_rationale: Jute retting, fibre separation, and processing are manual physical tasks deeply tied to rural Bangladesh. Essentially zero digital work product.

occupations.title: Fisherman (Inland & Coastal)
occupations.ai_rationale: Core work is physical net-casting and boat operation. Some AI-powered weather and fish-finding apps emerging, but the catch itself is entirely physical.

occupations.title: Poultry & Livestock Farmer
occupations.ai_rationale: Daily feeding, healthcare, and husbandry are physical tasks. AI diagnostic apps beginning to emerge for disease detection, but core role is manual.

occupations.title: Agricultural Day Laborer
occupations.ai_rationale: Seasonal wage labour involving planting, weeding, harvesting. Purely physical. No digital work output. Among lowest AI exposure occupations.

occupations.title: Shrimp & Aquaculture Farmer
occupations.ai_rationale: Managing shrimp ghers requires physical presence for feeding, water quality monitoring, and harvesting. Some IoT sensor adoption in export-oriented farms.

occupations.title: RMG Sewing Machine Operator
occupations.ai_rationale: Highly repetitive but requires manual dexterity and physical machine operation on factory floor. Some automation via advanced sewing robots exists but mass adoption in Bangladesh is limited by wage economics.

occupations.title: RMG Quality Control Inspector
occupations.ai_rationale: Physical inspection of garments is core but AI-powered vision systems for defect detection are increasingly adopted in RMG factories. Role will shift toward supervising automated QC systems.

occupations.title: RMG Fabric Cutting Worker
occupations.ai_rationale: Cutting is physical and pattern-based. CAD/CAM cutting systems are used in mid-to-large factories. Digital exposure exists at the pattern-design stage, but physical cutting remains dominant.

occupations.title: RMG Production Supervisor
occupations.ai_rationale: Supervisors use ERP and production management software increasingly. AI tools for production scheduling and worker efficiency tracking are entering larger factories. Physical floor presence still essential.

occupations.title: Textile Mill Spinner / Weaver
occupations.ai_rationale: Operating spinning and weaving machinery is physical. Some digital monitoring of machine parameters, but core work is mechanical.

occupations.title: Construction / Civil Works Laborer
occupations.ai_rationale: Manual brick-laying, concrete mixing, and scaffolding work. Entirely physical and site-bound. AI cannot substitute for physical construction labour.

occupations.title: Mason & Carpenter (Construction)
occupations.ai_rationale: Skilled trade requiring physical dexterity on construction sites. Some use of digital drawings/plans. Core craft is manual and location-bound.

occupations.title: Electrician & Plumber
occupations.ai_rationale: Installation and repair work is physical and site-specific. AI troubleshooting assistants may help diagnostics, but hands-on work cannot be digitized.

occupations.title: Factory Machine Operator (General)
occupations.ai_rationale: Operating machinery in pharma, food processing or ceramics sectors. Some smart monitoring systems exist but physical operation dominates.

occupations.title: Shipbuilding & Repair Worker
occupations.ai_rationale: Heavy manual metalwork and welding in shipyards. Some digital design tools used by engineers but workers perform physical construction tasks.

occupations.title: Retail Shop Worker / Salesperson
occupations.ai_rationale: Customer-facing retail requires local presence. Larger shops use POS systems. E-commerce growth is gradually shifting some retail activities to digital platforms.

occupations.title: Rickshaw / Van Puller
occupations.ai_rationale: Purely physical transport work. Cannot be automated or digitized. Rickshaws are a fundamental part of Bangladesh urban transport.

occupations.title: Ride-Sharing Driver (Pathao/Shohoz)
occupations.ai_rationale: Uses smartphone app platform for dispatch and payment. Work product is physical driving but algorithmic management via AI platforms determines income and work patterns significantly.

occupations.title: Truck / Bus / Tempo Driver
occupations.ai_rationale: Driving on Bangladesh's road network requires local navigation expertise. AI autonomous vehicles are decades away from Bangladesh roads.

occupations.title: Hotel, Restaurant & Food Service Worker
occupations.ai_rationale: Food preparation and in-person service are physical. Some digitization of ordering in large restaurants but street food and mid-range dining remains manual.

occupations.title: Domestic Worker / Household Help
occupations.ai_rationale: Physical housework—cooking, cleaning, childcare—cannot be digitized. Entirely in-person and human-to-human. Among the lowest AI exposure categories.

occupations.title: Mobile Banking Agent (bKash/Nagad)
occupations.ai_rationale: Agents use smartphone platforms and digital systems. AI-powered fraud detection and automated workflows are actively transforming this space. Physical cash handling remains for now.

occupations.title: Microfinance Field Officer (NGO/MFI)
occupations.ai_rationale: Uses mobile apps and digital forms for loan disbursement and collection. AI credit scoring is increasingly used by MFIs. Field visits remain essential but digital tools are pervasive.

occupations.title: Pharmacy Salesperson / Drug Dispenser
occupations.ai_rationale: Dispensing medicines requires physical presence. AI-powered drug interaction checkers and digital prescriptions are growing but full digitization is limited by regulatory and literacy constraints.

occupations.title: Beauty Salon & Barber Worker
occupations.ai_rationale: Entirely physical hands-on personal grooming service. Cannot be replaced or augmented by AI in meaningful ways.

occupations.title: Software Engineer / Developer
occupations.ai_rationale: Work product is entirely digital code and documentation. AI coding assistants (GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT) already substantially augment developer productivity. Remote work possible. Among the highest AI exposure occupations globally.

occupations.title: Data Analyst / Business Analyst
occupations.ai_rationale: Data analysis work—SQL queries, Python scripts, BI dashboards—is entirely digital. AI and LLMs can already perform much of this work. Role shifting toward interpretation and communication of AI-generated insights.

occupations.title: IT Support & Helpdesk Technician
occupations.ai_rationale: Remote troubleshooting and ticket resolution is highly automatable by AI chatbots and diagnostic systems. Physical hardware replacement requires presence. Mix of digital and physical exposure.

occupations.title: Freelance Digital Worker (Upwork/Fiverr)
occupations.ai_rationale: Bangladesh has one of the world's largest freelancer communities. Tasks like data entry, graphic design, and content writing are being directly automated by generative AI tools.

occupations.title: Bank Officer / Financial Analyst
occupations.ai_rationale: Financial analysis, loan processing, and report generation are largely digital. AI models for credit scoring and document processing are actively adopted by Bangladeshi banks. KYC still requires physical presence.

occupations.title: BCS Administration Officer (Govt.)
occupations.ai_rationale: Document processing and report writing are digital but citizen service delivery requires physical presence. e-governance initiatives expanding digital work but regulatory and bureaucratic processes remain paper-heavy.

occupations.title: Primary School Teacher
occupations.ai_rationale: Core teaching requires physical presence and human interaction with children. AI tutoring tools may supplement but cannot replace the teacher-student relationship, especially in rural Bangladesh with limited connectivity.

occupations.title: Secondary & HSC Teacher
occupations.ai_rationale: Growing use of digital content and online tutoring platforms. AI can generate lesson plans and explanations but classroom instruction is still physically delivered in Bangladesh's education system.

occupations.title: University / College Professor
occupations.ai_rationale: Research output is entirely digital—papers, reports, grant applications. AI tools for literature review, writing, and data analysis are transforming academic work. Teaching component still requires physical presence.

occupations.title: Medical Doctor (MBBS / Specialist)
occupations.ai_rationale: Medical diagnosis has significant AI augmentation potential but physical examination, surgery, and patient trust require in-person presence. AI diagnostic tools (radiology AI, ECG analysis) growing in Bangladeshi hospitals.

occupations.title: Nurse & Paramedic
occupations.ai_rationale: Bedside care, medication administration, and patient monitoring are physical. AI can support scheduling and documentation but direct care cannot be digitized.

occupations.title: Journalist / Media Professional
occupations.ai_rationale: Writing, editing, and content creation are highly exposed to LLM automation. Digital journalism workflows are fully digital. AI can already generate news summaries, translations, and routine reporting.

occupations.title: Accountant / Auditor
occupations.ai_rationale: Bookkeeping, tax filing, and audit reports are highly automatable digital tasks. AI accounting tools already handle routine transactions. Role shifting toward financial advisory and compliance interpretation.

occupations.title: Lawyer / Legal Professional
occupations.ai_rationale: Legal research, contract drafting, and document review are highly digital and LLM-exposed. Court appearances and client negotiation require physical presence. AI legal tools growing rapidly.

occupations.title: NGO & Development Worker
occupations.ai_rationale: Report writing, data collection, and grant applications are digital. Field work and community facilitation require physical presence. Bangladesh's massive NGO sector (BRAC, Grameen, ASA) increasingly uses digital MIS tools.

occupations.title: Civil & Electrical Engineer
occupations.ai_rationale: Design work uses digital tools (AutoCAD, BIM) that AI is beginning to augment. Site supervision, inspection, and construction management require physical presence.

occupations.title: Call Center / BPO Agent
occupations.ai_rationale: Conversational AI and chatbots are directly replacing call center agents globally. Bangladesh's growing BPO sector faces high disruption risk as LLMs handle routine customer service interactions in Bangla and English.

occupations.title: Overseas Migrant Worker
occupations.ai_rationale: Construction workers, cleaners, domestic helpers, and drivers abroad perform physical labour in Gulf and Malaysian economies. AI cannot substitute for physical construction, cleaning, or driving in these contexts yet.

occupations.title: Garments Merchandiser / Export Officer
occupations.ai_rationale: Communication with buyers, order tracking, and quality documentation are primarily digital. AI tools for email drafting, specification checking, and ERP optimization are applicable. Factory visits still required.
